<!--
  Curated release highlights for the NEXT release. Maintainer-edited.
  `scripts/build-release-notes.mjs` prepends this verbatim as the "## Highlights"
  section of both the GitHub Release notes and the Version Packages PR body.
  Keep it to the few most important user-facing changes (newest release).
  Update (or clear) this file when cutting a new release.
-->
- **In-process transport (flagship feature)** — call services running on the same `createServer()` instance without a network hop. New public API `createLocalTransport`, `server.client()` (auto-routes in-process vs remote), `server.localClient()`, and `server.hasService()`. The local path runs the full server interceptor chain (validation, authorization, OpenTelemetry) and keeps 1-to-1 behavioural parity with the HTTP/gRPC transport. Strictly additive. ([#117](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/117))
- **Service catalog — declarative cross-service calls** — `defineService` / `defineLazyService` are the new canonical service registration (`createServer({ services })` takes `ServiceDefinition[]`; the legacy `(router) => void` form is removed). Catalog primitives (`defineCatalog`, `mergeCatalogs`, `ServiceCatalog`), a synchronous `RemoteResolver` with built-in helpers (`singleTransportResolver`, `mapResolver`), and env-driven `enabledServices` helpers standardize hybrid monolith ↔ microservices topology in boot code, not proto.
- **EventBus with pluggable broker adapters** — proto-first pub/sub with a middleware pipeline and DLQ, plus adapters for NATS JetStream, Kafka/Redpanda, and Redis Streams; integrated into the server lifecycle via `createServer({ eventBus })`. ([#45](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/45))
- **AMQP / RabbitMQ adapter** — new `@connectum/events-amqp` package: topic-exchange wildcard routing, durable competing-consumer queues, dead-letter integration, external AsyncAPI-style contracts, automatic recovery, and reliable per-message publishing with typed errors. ([#65](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/65), [#140](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/140))
- **Authentication & authorization** — JWT/JWKS auth, proto-based authorization from `.proto` options, gateway/session interceptors with LRU caching, and client-side bearer/gateway interceptors. ([#30](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/30), [#24](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/24), [#92](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/92))
- **OpenTelemetry instrumentation** — unary and streaming RPC tracing/metrics aligned with OTel RPC semantic conventions, `service.instance.id` and custom resource attributes, and OTel SDK upgraded to stable 2.8.0. ([#147](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/147), [#146](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/146), [#24](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/24))
- **BREAKING — bidi-streaming transport validation** — `server.start()` now fails fast with `TransportValidationError` (`CONNECTUM_UNSUPPORTED_STREAMING_TRANSPORT`) when bidirectional-streaming methods are registered on a transport that cannot serve them (e.g. the default plaintext HTTP/1.1), instead of hanging silently at runtime. Tunable via `transportValidation: "error" | "warn" | "off"`. ([#141](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/141))
- **BREAKING — interceptor defaults are now explicit opt-in** — `createDefaultInterceptors()` no longer silently enables resilience (`timeout`, `bulkhead`, `circuitBreaker`, `retry`) or `serializer`; only `errorHandler` and `validation` stay on by default. The circuit breaker also now trips on infrastructure errors only. Enable each explicitly. ([#138](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/138), [#77](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/77))
- **BREAKING — minimum Node.js raised to 22.13.0** — Node.js 20 reached end-of-life and the `engines.node` floor is now `>=22.13.0` across all packages. ([#129](https://github.com/Connectum-Framework/connectum/pull/129))
